entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop being studied for technical efficiency and technology gaps in Uganda

entities.name: UGANDA
entities.description: The country where the study on sorghum plots was conducted

entities.name: TECHNICAL EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A measure of how effectively plot managers use their resource endowments

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY GAPS
entities.description: The difference between the actual technical efficiency and the potential efficiency of the metafrontier

entities.name: STOCHASTIC METAFRONTIER APPROACH
entities.description: A statistical method used to assess technical efficiency and technology gaps across different groups

entities.name: TWO LIMIT-TOBIT MODEL
entities.description: A regression model used to analyze the determinants of technical efficiency

entities.name: LIRA
entities.description: A district in Uganda where sorghum plots were analyzed

entities.name: SERERE
entities.description: A district in Uganda where sorghum plots were analyzed

entities.name: KUMI
entities.description: A district in Uganda where sorghum plots were analyzed

entities.name: RESOURCE ENDOWMENT
entities.description: The available resources that influence the technical efficiency of plot managers

entities.name: GENDER
entities.description: The classification of plot managers as male, female, or jointly-managed

entities.name: AGE
entities.description: A demographic factor that positively influenced technical efficiency

entities.name: DISTANCE TO PLOT
entities.description: The physical distance from the manager to the plot, which positively influenced technical efficiency

entities.name: FARMER GROUP MEMBERSHIP
entities.description: Participation in farmer groups, which positively influenced technical efficiency

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD SIZE
entities.description: The number of people in the household, which negatively influenced technical efficiency

entities.name: YEARS OF FARMING SORGHUM
entities.description: The experience of the farmer in growing sorghum, which negatively influenced technical efficiency

entities.name: ACCESS TO CREDIT
entities.description: The ability to obtain loans, which negatively influenced technical efficiency
